plan_summary.plan_name: Denmark DKK to EUR Adoption (Builder's Blueprint)
plan_summary.primary_goal: Achieve full Denmark currency transition from DKK to EUR within 4-8 years via sequenced ERM II compliance, referendum mandate, and operational conversion.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Quantifies budget adequacy (transition cost vs envelope), inflation erosion of the multi-year budget, contingency runway against cash-logistics shock, and public-information funding coverage. Does not model EU treaty repeal, referendum mandate, or ECB convergence approval — those are unmodelled gates.

key_values.label: Total national transition budget floor
key_values.comment: Stated DKK 15-25B reference range over 6 years. Floor used as conservative envelope for affordability gate.
key_values.source_text: Total transition budget reference range: DKK 15-25 Billion over 6 years

key_values.label: Total transition duration
key_values.comment: Project spans 4-8 years from political decision; 6 is the budget reference horizon used for inflation erosion math.
key_values.source_text: Project spans 4 to 8 years from decision to full euro use.

key_values.label: Ring-fenced contingency reserve
key_values.comment: Ring-fenced reserve for unbudgeted security/logistics shocks during dual circulation; expected to absorb DKK 100M overhead risk.
key_values.source_text: Required ring-fenced contingency capital: DKK 50 million plus 5% annual inflation adjustment

key_values.label: Cash logistics shock cost (dual circulation extension)
key_values.comment: Stress-test magnitude: dual-circulation logistics failure adds ~DKK 100M overhead. Tested against the contingency reserve.
key_values.source_text: Cash logistics failure during 30-60 day dual circulation risks up to DKK 100M overhead cost.

key_values.label: Public information funding share of total budget
key_values.comment: 0.5% of total transition budget mandated for public info campaigns; critical to referendum mitigation.
key_values.source_text: Public information funding required: 0.5% of total national transition budget

key_values.label: Annual inflation excess over baseline
key_values.comment: Stress-test scenario: annual inflation 2pp above baseline erodes ~DKK 2.2B of real budget value over 6 years.
key_values.source_text: If average annual inflation exceeds baseline by 2 pp, real budget value reduces by approx. DKK 2.2 Billion

derived_questions.question: Does the planned inflation indexation review preserve real budget value against the +2pp shock scenario?
derived_questions.why_it_matters: Inflation erosion of ~DKK 2.2B over 6 years could force scaling delays or capital calls if indexation is incomplete.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Estimated actual transition cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Compared against the stated budget envelope to test whether the floor of DKK 15B covers actual IT, legal, cash logistics, and communications spend.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Bottom-up sum of comparable EU adoption programs (Baltic precedents) scaled to Danish population and system complexity.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Baseline annual inflation rate
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Compounded over the 6-year horizon to compute real budget value and the inflation-erosion shortfall.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Use ECB/Danish central bank CPI baseline (~1.5%) as anchor; bound by ECB target band.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Estimated required public-information campaign cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Compared against the 0.5%-of-budget allocation to test whether mandated comms funding covers required campaign scope.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Benchmark from comparable national referendum/EU-accession ad spend per capita, scaled to Denmark.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Real value of transition budget after inflation
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Quantifies inflation erosion on the multi-year budget — the premortem's load-bearing financial stability constraint.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Budget envelope surplus after inflation
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the stated DKK 15B floor (in real terms) covers the estimated bottom-up cost of executing the transition.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Contingency reserve buffer after cash-logistics shock
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the DKK 50M ring-fenced reserve covers the stated DKK 100M dual-circulation overhead shock.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Public information funding surplus
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the 0.5% mandated allocation covers required campaign scope — referendum mitigation depends on this.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Combined financial viability surplus
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Worst-of-three gate: each pressure draws on a different pool (capital envelope vs contingency escrow vs comms allocation), so min() — not sum — is the right aggregator.

unmodelled_gates.label: EU treaty opt-out repeal agreement
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Requires unanimous EU agreement / Extraordinary Council protocol amendment; the model treats this as binary and does not test it.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: 12-24 month delay or project termination; no legal path to EUR adoption.

unmodelled_gates.label: Danish national referendum mandate
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Binary Yes/No referendum is the constitutional gate; failure ends the program and triggers DKK 500M-1B sunk-cost write-off.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Project termination; return to permanent opt-out; preparatory legislation auto-repealed.

unmodelled_gates.label: ECB convergence criteria approval
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Maastricht criteria (deficit, debt, inflation, interest rates) must be met during ERM II; pass/fail is determined by ECB, not by the model.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: 6-18 month delay; forced fiscal adjustments; referendum cannot be scheduled.

unmodelled_gates.label: Danmarks Nationalbank Eurosystem integration
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: TARGET2 / payment-system integration is a technical readiness gate the model assumes succeeds; integration failure adds 4-6 months remediation.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Inability to operate on Euro Day; isolation/non-compliance with Eurosystem.

unmodelled_gates.label: Joint Ministerial Consultative Body consensus velocity
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Quarterly consensus-based governance must avoid paralysis; bottlenecks could extend timeline beyond 8 years, but the model has no quantitative test.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Cascading delays across IT, contracts, and cash-logistics workstreams.
